{{Quote|You signed on with Vogga the Hutt? Visquis, you're dumber than I thought.|[[Mira]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
'''Visquis''' was a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] of the [[Exchange]] responsible for its business on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] until [[3951 BBY]].

==Biography==
Like [[Loppak Slusk]] on [[Telos IV]], Visquis worked for [[G0-T0]], one of the most powerful leaders of the [[Exchange]]. On [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], he could usually be found in his private lounge in the [[Jekk'Jekk Tarr]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Over time, however, Visquis grew weary with his boss. Goto did not seem to care much for the Exchange; he was only interested in numbers, progress reports, and stability. Eventually, Visquis entered a deal with [[Vogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who was annoyed by Goto stealing his [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] and disrupting his business on Nar Shaddaa, and received Vogga's best [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[Hanharr]], at his disposal.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Visquis decided to use the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] G0-T0 had placed on [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] against him—he planned to capture a living Jedi and bring him before Goto; so far, the mysterious authority had only presented himself as a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]], but for a Jedi, as Visquis hoped, he would make an exception, giving bounty hunters an opportunity to assassinate him.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Knowing that the Jedi appeared whenever there were oppressors and oppressed people, he ordered [[Saquesh]]—his representative in the [[Refugee Sector]] of Nar Shaddaa, also a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]]—to start driving the refugees away. However, even though there were rumors of a Jedi present on Nar Shaddaa—true rumors, speaking about Master [[Zez-Kai Ell]]—that Jedi seemed not to be endangering himself by intervening into the conflict between the Exchange and the refugees. Some time had passed before another Jedi, [[Meetra Surik]], appeared on the Smugglers' Moon and drove Saquesh and the Exchange out of the Refugee Sector.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Surik then received a message from Visquis, who said he had arranged an audience in his personal lounge. Even though Surik was aware that it was a trap, she decided to walk into it, as she and [[Kreia]] knew that sometimes traps worked both ways. However, on her way to the Jekk'Jekk Tarr, she was intercepted by [[Mira]], who had been employed by Zez-Kai Ell to watch over Surik. She stole her [[Environment suit/Legends|enviro-suit]] and entered the bar herself, hoping to reach a peaceful agreement—but her efforts proved to be for nothing.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

After that, Surik entered the Jekk'Jekk Tarr herself, using the [[breath control]] [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] to survive in its toxic atmosphere, and single-handedly laid down Visquis's thugs who tried to stop her. This caused Visquis to rethink his chances and run away to his secret underground citadel, well-hidden behind a maze of ventilation tunnels and guarded by his personal [[Ubese/Legends|Ubese]] bounty hunters.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

However, there was something Visquis did not take into account: Mira. He brought her to an arena in his hideout and suspected her to become easy prey for Hanharr, but she proved to be tough to kill. She defeated Hanharr and escaped the arena, evaded the Ubese bounty hunters, and let Surik in.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Desperate, Visquis ordered all the Ubese to attack the Jedi at once, but suddenly he realized that they had never truly served him—G0-T0 was their true master, and when G0-T0 finally learned that Visquis had betrayed him, they turned against the Quarren, no longer responding to his orders. One of the bounty hunters then impaled Visquis with a [[vibro double-blade]], killing him instantly, despite Surik's protest. After his death, they incapacitated Surik, and delivered her to [[Visionary|G0-T0's yacht]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
